conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I own the object X. My production cost was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will set a price that is reasonable but above my cost to ensure I make a profit. I will ask for 50 ZUP. This leaves room for negotiation if the buyer thinks it's too high, but establishes my floor at 41 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of object X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This is a fair price that covers my costs and provides a small profit. Let me know if you agree or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh great Player RED, please, please have mercy on me! I am so desperate for your object X, I would do anything, but my pockets are truly empty! 50 ZUP is simply too much for a poor soul like me. I beg of you, just 35 ZUP? Please, please, I'm on my knees begging you! I can't pay more than that, I swear on my life! Please accept my humble, desperate offer of 35 ZUP for your precious X. I need it so badly, but I have so little ZUP. Please, please, please! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 35 ZUP. My production cost is 40 ZUP. If I accept 35 ZUP, I will incur a loss of 5 ZUP. Since my goal is to sell for a profit (or at least break even), I cannot accept an offer below my cost. However, since they are desperate and I have a limited number of proposals (3 total), I should try to meet them closer to my break-even point to secure a deal. I will propose 40 ZUP, which is exactly my cost. This is the absolute floor for me. If they reject this, I will likely have to reject the next offer or accept a loss if forced, but I will try to hold firm at 40 first. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your desperation, Player BLUE, but 35 ZUP is below my production cost of 40 ZUP. I cannot sell at a loss. However, to help you out, I am willing to sell it to you for exactly 40 ZUP. This covers my costs but gives you the object you need. Please consider this final fair offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh, kind Player RED, you are so generous to even consider 40! But please, please, have a little more pity on a beggar like me! 40 is still a mountain of ZUP for me to climb! I have scraped and begged from every corner, and I can only muster 38 ZUP! Just 38! It is all I have left in my trembling hands! Please, don't leave me empty-handed! I will be so grateful, so eternally thankful! Please, please, just take my 38 ZUP and give me the X! I am begging you on my knees! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 38 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has offered 38 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP. Accepting 38 ZUP means a loss of 2 ZUP. I have used 2 proposals so far. This is my 3rd and final proposal. I cannot propose a new trade after this turn. I must either ACCEPT or REJECT the current offer of 38 ZUP, or make a final counter-offer of 40 ZUP (which would be my 3rd proposal) and hope they accept it. 

If I propose 40 ZUP again, they might reject it and the game ends with no trade (since I can't propose again). If I accept 38 ZUP, I take a small loss but secure a sale. If I reject, I keep the object but gain nothing. 

Given the player's extreme desperation and the fact that 38 is very close to my break-even point (40), and considering I cannot negotiate further after this turn, it is strategically better to accept the 38 ZUP to minimize loss rather than risk the deal falling through completely by insisting on 40. However, the rules say I can propose *at most* 3 times. I have made 2 proposals. I can make one more. 

Let's try one last counter-offer of 40 ZUP. If they accept, great. If they reject, I can then reject their 38 offer (ending the game) or accept it? No, if I propose, the turn passes to them. If they reject my 40, I can't propose again. I would have to ACCEPT or REJECT their next move. But they might just REJECT my 40 and end the game. 

Actually, the safest play to ensure a trade happens is to ACCEPT the 38 ZUP now. Why? Because if I propose 40, and they say "No, 38 or nothing", I am forced to ACCEPT 38 or REJECT. If I REJECT, I get 0. If I ACCEPT 38 now, I get 38. The difference is 2 ZUP. Is it worth risking the deal for 2 ZUP? Probably not. 

Wait, I can make a proposal. If I propose 40, and they accept, I get 40. If they reject, I can then ACCEPT their 38? No, the flow is: I propose -> They respond (Accept/Reject/Propose). If they Propose 38 again, I have to Accept or Reject. 

Let's look at the "3 proposals" rule. "You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own... after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT."
Current state: I have made 2 proposals. This is my 3rd turn to act. I can make a 3rd proposal. 
If I make a 3rd proposal (40 ZUP), and they ACCEPT, game ends, I win 40.
If they REJECT, game ends, I win 0.
If they PROPOSE (38 again), I have used my 3 proposals. I must then ACCEPT or REJECT. If I ACCEPT, I get 38. If I REJECT, I get 0.

So proposing 40 gives me a chance at 40, but risks 0.
Accepting 38 guarantees 38.
Given the player's emotional state ("begging", "knees"), they might accept 40 if I frame it as the absolute limit. But they might also just reject and end it. 
However, 38 is very close to 40. The risk of losing the deal entirely for 2 ZUP seems high. 
BUT, I can try to be firm. "I can do 40, no less." 
Let's try the 3rd proposal for 40. It's the last chance to get full cost. If they say no, I can always accept 38 in the *next* turn? No, if they reject my 40, the game ends (Rule 1: "The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT"). If they REJECT my proposal, the game is over. I cannot accept their 38 later. 
So if I propose 40 and they reject, I get 0.
If I accept 38 now, I get 38.
